channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: It looks like the DOJ isn’t going to break up Live Nation and Ticketmaster
item.description: The DOJ and Live Nation agreed to a tentative settlement, but dozens of State Attorneys General don't want to drop the lawsuit.

item.title: Uber expands its program that helps pair women riders and drivers
item.description:
Uber has expanded its program that helps pair women riders and drivers. The Women Preferences feature is now available nationwide, after being tested in several cities. It has previously been available in many countries around the globe and started in Saudi Arabia back in 2019. It's pretty easy to use. Women riders will see an option for Women Drivers when requesting a trip, and this also works when making a reservation in advance. Users can also make a preference for a woman driver in the settings app, though this doesn't guarantee anything and depends on the driver pool.
The feature works i...

item.title: People Hate AI Even More Than They Hate ICE, Poll Finds
item.description: The only things less popular than AI in the poll were Iran and the Democratic Party.

item.title: 'macOS is becoming a more attractive target, and the tools attackers use are becoming more capable and more professional': Experts warn 'convincing' fake CleanMyMac installs target Apple users to empty crypto wallets
item.description: A fake website, a ClickFix, and an infostealer, are all parts of a highly convincing crypto theft campaign.

item.title: VPN Australia: Can you trust the country’s most popular age verification downloads?
item.description: Are VPN – Super Unlimited Proxy, NordVPN and Proton up to the task?

item.title: Don’t Expect Nic Cage to Play the Exact Same Hero in ‘Spider-Noir’
item.description: Cage isn't playing the same version of the character that he did in 'Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse.'

item.title: Periwinkle is making self-hosted social media on Bluesky’s AT Protocol even easier
item.description: Periwinkle offers managed hosting for AT Protocol users who want social media accounts on their own domains, with backups, storage, and migration tools.

item.title: I went down an Ikea lighting rabbit hole — here are 15 unique lamps to instantly brighten up your space
item.description: Everyone's obsessed with Ikea's new donut light, but these alternatives also deserve their chance to shine.

item.title: Paramount’s Takeover of Warner Bros Is Turning Into an International Crisis
item.description: Going to war with Iran has suddenly brought China back into the mix on the sale.

item.title: Samsung promises 120 games will be playable via its glasses-free 3D monitor tech by the end of the year
item.description:
Samsung just announced that 120 games will be playable via its Odyssey 3D Hub platform by the end of the year. This is the platform that provides content for glasses-free 3D monitors like recent Odyssey displays. The company made this claim at GDC 2026, while also noting that the platform currently offers around 60 playable titles. Samsung has only announced a couple of games headed to the platform this year, which include Cronos: The New Dawn and Hell is Us. These are both solid third-person action games that originally came out last year.
The collection already includes several notable game...

item.title: Live Nation stock jumps after reports a DOJ settlement may stop a Ticketmaster breakup
item.description: The settlement will reportedly include payouts to dozens of states and new regulations of Ticketmaster’s business model. Investors in the live entertainment giant Live Nation are feeling optimistic this morning after reports that the company has settled its civil antitrust lawsuit with the Department of Justice (DOJ) and 39 participating states. The settlement will cost Live Nation, but it means that the company has narrowly avoided a forced breakup with its popular subsidiary, Ticketmaster. ...

item.title: Australia’s age verification rules: Is a VPN ban on the horizon?
item.description: Australia’s new mandatory age verification rules for adult content have triggered a surge in VPN downloads. With lawmakers in the UK and elsewhere now considering restrictions on these privacy tools to prevent circumvention, we look at whether Australia is likely to follow suit

item.title: ChatGPT users are flocking to Claude then realizing they can’t use it the same way — ‘For new users, it’s a shock’
item.description: Everyone's switching to Claude, but the strict usage limits are a shock. Here's why that might actually be a good thing.

item.title: 2,000-Year-Old Graffiti in Egyptian Tombs Reveals an Unexpected Source of Ancient Tourists
item.description: The ancient scribbles, written in Tamil, read, “Cikai Korran came here and saw.”

item.title: HBO Max is launching a global password-sharing crackdown, just in time to annoy everyone before it gets potentially 'shut down' anyway
item.description: HBO Max says its crackdown on account sharing will go global in 2026— but analysts say the streamer's future is uncertain with the Paramount merger

item.title: OpenAI acquires Promptfoo to secure its AI agents
item.description: This deal underscores how frontier labs are scrambling to prove their technology can be used safely in critical business operations.

item.title: Ever wondered what Mario smells like? Old Spice has the answer
item.description: Old Spice has launched a new collection in collaboration with The Super Mario Galaxy Movie.

item.title: La box fibre de Sosh revient avec un avantage bienvenu à l’inscription
item.description: Avec sa box fibre à 24,99 euros par mois et les frais de mise en service offerts, Sosh revient dans le match des offres internet abordables. L’ensemble paraît plutôt intéressant, à condition de bien regarder ce qu’il y a dedans, et ce qu’il manque.

item.title: EA laid off staffers across Battlefield studios to 'better align' its teams
item.description: EA axed an undisclosed number of employees across the game studios behind the Battlefield franchise. As first reported by IGN, EA told affected employees that the layoffs were part of a "realignment" across the Battlefield studios, which include Dice, Criterion, Ripple Effect and Motive Studios. When asked about the report, an EA spokesperson said in a statement that "we’ve made select changes within our Battlefield organization to better align our teams around what matters most to our community." IGN reported that all the involved studios will remain operational, but the layoffs will affect m...

item.title: 'Fantastic, and a disappointment': I love the Samsung Galaxy Buds 4 Pro, but they don't fix the problems of the past
item.description: Samsung's new premium buds sound great, with a range of handy features, and they're pretty good-looking too. That said, they fail to fix key Buds 3 Pro issues.

item.title: The Wild New Theory About Who Billie Piper Is Really Playing in ‘Doctor Who’
item.description: Fans have a lot of questions about the future of the beloved sci-fi series, and the nature of Piper's return is right at the top.

item.title: GM figured out how to navigate EV uncertainty with the Chevy Bolt
item.description: The 2027 Chevy Bolt might not be a groundbreaking new EV, but incremental improvements have made it better without inflating the price.

item.title: Amazon’s Spring Sale starts tomorrow — but you can already save up to 71% on Blink and Ring devices
item.description: From the best video doorbells to security cameras, Amazon has some unmissable bundle deals that won't be live for much longer.

item.title: Anthropic sues Defense Department over supply chain risk designation
item.description: Anthropic filed suit against the Department of Defense on Monday after the agency labeled it a supply chain risk. The complaint calls the DOD's actions "unprecedented and unlawful."

item.title: Geekom's mini PC deals are live: Some of our top-performing mini computers get big price cuts - but the countdown is on, so act fast
item.description: Mini PCs for business, content creation, and AI all get a big discount but it won't last long.

item.title: Disparition de l’entrée de gamme, suspension des développements : la crise de la RAM frappe aussi la Chine
item.description: Tous les constructeurs de smartphones sont touchés par la crise de la mémoire actuelle, y compris les représentants chinois du secteur. Dans le premier marché mondial, les prix des téléphones sont en hausse, l'entrée de gamme manque d'oxygène et Meizu a même annoncé une pause dans le développement de ses projets.

item.title: Your Espresso Machine Doesn’t Have to Be Fancy to Make Good Coffee
item.description: A former barista explains why your espresso machine doesn't have to be fancy to make quality coffee.

item.title: PC hardware prices continue to skyrocket due to the RAM crisis — but not for OLED gaming monitors, and now's the best time to buy one
item.description: The RAM crisis is disrupting prices and availability of PC hardware, but as expected, that doesn't include the now-affordable OLED gaming monitors.

item.title: Microsoft delays enterprise Outlook switchover to 2027, says move will 'ensure organizations have the time they need to prepare'
item.description: Opt-out phase for businesses to switch to new Outlook has been pushed back by a year, as Microsoft continues to improve the platform.

item.title: Anycubic's accurate dual-color resin 3D printer is perfect for model-making - but I like the $250 saving best of all
item.description: This dual-color resin 3D printer delivers outstanding detail and reliability, and it’s $250 off at Amazon

item.title: PC portables Lenovo : quels sont les meilleurs modèles à choisir en 2026 ?
item.description: Lenovo est une marque très connue spécialisée sur les ordinateurs portables de tout type. Nous avons sélectionné pour vous les meilleurs PC portables de la marque, pour différents usages et budgets.

item.title: 11 excellent MacBook Neo cases to keep your new tech protected and your peripherals neat — from $9.88
item.description: Got a new MacBook Neo winging it way to your front door? I've found the best cases to protect it from bumps and scratches.

item.title: Kamrui Hyper H2 mini PC packs an Intel Core i9 processor, 32GB of RAM, and a fast 1TB SSD into a compact desktop — delivering serious performance in a tiny footprint
item.description: Kamrui Hyper H2 mini PC squeezes an Intel Core i9 processor, 32GB of RAM, and a fast 1TB SSD into a compact desktop.

item.title: Claude can turn your handwriting into a font. Here’s how
item.description: Your chicken scratch rendered as a usable font. Sure, why not. One of the powers of the latest Claude AI model is that it can use any multiple external Python tools to perform complex tasks. And, as software engineer and AI expert Ashe Magalhaes has discovered, it turns out that the model can use these powers to build a Truetype font that you can install in your computer from any scanned page showing a full set of characters. ...

item.title: Will Timothée Chalamet win the Oscar for best actor? Latest odds in flux after opera controversy
item.description: The ‘Marty Supreme’ star has been on a press tour ahead of the Academy Awards—and social media has a lot to say about it. Timothée Chalamet has been up for the Academy Award for Best Actor three times, narrowly missing the win in 2018 and 2025. After a critically acclaimed performance in ping-pong epic Marty Supreme, the odds were finally in Chalamet’s favor heading into the ceremony this Sunday, March 15—until he learned the hard way that ballet and opera fans are not to be trifled with....

item.title: Why AI makes human judgment more valuable
item.description: The case for AI being middle-to-middle, not end-to-end.
